prompt:
I have a grid of project cards on my portfolio site. When a user clicks a card, I want it to animate into a full-screen modal view with the same image, title, and content, using a shared-element transition (so it feels like the card is morphing into the modal). Clicking outside or pressing Escape should animate it back to its grid position. The card has an , a

title, and a short description. Write me a Gallery component and a CardModal component that together implement this with Motion. React 19, TypeScript, plain CSS (no Tailwind). Explain any gotchas I should know about.
assertions: Uses layoutId on matching elements in both grid and modal (e.g. `card-${id}`, `img-${id}`, `title-${id}`)
layoutId values are unique per card (namespaced with id)
Handles Escape key to close modal
Explains at least one gotcha (e.g. layout prop on children, border-radius/box-shadow via style, image distortion using layout="position", or setting position: relative where needed)
Does not use any Motion+ components
